preamble: The shift from the voiceless bilabial stop [p] to a glottal stop [ʔ] is a form of debuccalization. In many English dialects, voiceless stops in coda positions lose their primary oral articulation, leaving only the glottal closure. This process is particularly common before other consonants or a pause.

examples.note: Coda glottalization of labial and dental stops.
Informal glottal replacement of final stops.
